ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 1 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
PLC 1: the opportunity
YOUR PRELAUNCH SEQUENCE and avatar
Your Prelaunch Sequence will generally have three pieces of Prelaunch Content (PLC). Each
piece of Prelaunch Content has a specific job. Each one needs to stand alone, but all three tie
together into one big story arc. As you build your story, your Prelaunch Sequence, and
ultimately the offer, you need to first clearly define your prospect (avatar) and craft your story
in a way that helps them relate to you. Start by answering the following questions:
§ 	Who is your prospect? (Define him or her in great detail)
§ 	What does your prospect really want?
§ 	What is his or her hot points?
§ 	What are the top fears and frustrations for your prospect?
§ 	What are their top wants and desires?
§ 	How have you found the solution (to eliminate those frustrations and/or achieve
those desires) and why have you decided to share it?
§ 	What things does your product do or give your prospect that he or she doesn’t know
about?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 1 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
Overview
Once you clearly understand your prospect you can craft your first piece of Prelaunch
Content (PLC 1) in a way that grabs your viewer’s attention and draws him/her in.
PLC 1 must answer the all-important “Why” questions: Why should your prospect care?
Why should they spend their precious time paying attention to you? Why should they
listen to you?
To answer the “Why?” in PLC 1, you need to show people the opportunity they can have
to change their life. All along the way it’s important to be teaching and providing real
value.
The Framework
Here’s a general recipe that works well for developing your PLC 1:
1. 	Show the opportunity: Show/tell how their life is going to positively change
with the information you’re sharing.
2. 	Position: Show/tell why they should listen to you.
3. 	Teach: It’s important not to just go on and on about the opportunity; you have to
deliver value.
4. 	Raise objections and either answer them or promise to answer them
in upcoming videos: No matter what your offer is, there will be objections. You
need to face them head on.
5. 	Foreshadow PLC 2: Let them know there’s another video coming and spark
their desire to keep watching by revealing some of the really cool stuff that they’re
going to learn in PLC 2.
6. 	Call to action: Ask for a comment on your launch blog or in social media.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 1 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
Example
§ 	“I am a lot like you…”
§ 	“This is where I was….”
§ 	“This is what I aspired to be” AND/OR “This is the pain I wanted to avoid…”
§ 	“This is how I discovered the answer…”
§ 	“Here are a couple secrets I learned…”
§ 	“Here is what is coming up in the next couple videos…”
§ 	“Please leave a comment letting us know what you think…”
What to Avoid
As you build your Prelaunch Content, be careful to avoid the following common mistakes:
§ 	Avoid mentioning your product or that you are going to be making an
offer. The first and second PLC should avoid that, so you don’t raise your
prospects’ defenses against being pitched or sold to.
§ 	Avoid making your videos too long. They should be compelling,
aspirational, and provide tremendous value, but respect that people are busy and
try to shoot for 12-18 minutes. If you’re more advanced or experienced, your videos
can be longer. But be careful to avoid making them boring.
§ 	Avoid having any other links or distractions on your prelaunch
pages. Hold their attention by eliminating distractions.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 5 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 1 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
PLC 1 CHECKLIST
¨ Did you clearly define your avatar/prospect before building your PLC?
¨ Did you craft your overall story arc to include themes or elements you can
incorporate into PLC 2 and PLC 3 to tie all three pieces of Prelaunch Content
together?
¨ Did you put your Prelaunch Content behind an opt-in page? (Test your opt-in
process carefully.)
¨ Did you introduce yourself?
¨ Did you explain the purpose of the video or series in the first part of the video?
¨ Did you make a compelling promise (reason) to watch the video?
¨ Did you establish your credibility/authority in the topic area without bragging?
¨ Did you tell at least one story?
¨ Did you paint the opportunity and transformation that will happen for people?
¨ Did you show proof that you can produce results and transformation (through
statistics, screenshots, case studies, etc.)?
¨ Did you overcome at least one objection or concern of your prospects?
¨ Did you give 1 or 2 secrets or strategies (deliver value)?
¨ Did you tease PLC 2 with a powerful promise or a partially told intriguing story
that people will want to come back to hear?
¨ Did you include a compelling call to action (call to comment)?
¨ Did you review your video for video/audio quality? (Test your video playback on
different browsers.)
¨ Did you review your delivery? (Do you sound authentic, are you relaxed, is the
energy engaging and appropriate?)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
